spec_match.rationale: The builder correctly formats the combined cross-origin, integrity, and referrer-policy URL request modifier syntax across multiple CSS contexts.

interactive.rationale: The builder features checkboxes, text fields, select dropdowns, context tabs, and a copy-to-clipboard button that update the output block dynamically.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligned perfectly with the parent description of an interactive tool to construct and view generated modifiers.

edge_cases.rationale: The tool does not perform live parsing validation on the user's customized combinations, so malformed inputs or unsupported combinations are shown as valid.

references.rationale: The parent index has the ChromeStatus links, but this page lacks direct references to MDN or specs.

design_system.rationale: Perfect design system conformance with appropriate font scaling, borders, and button structures.

openQuestions.title: Add live browser parser validation to the builder
openQuestions.detail: We can significantly improve the tool's robustness by attempting to insert the generated CSS rule into a temporary CSSStyleSheet via CSSStyleSheet.insertRule() inside the render loop. If it throws a DOMException, we can display a clear 'Syntax Error' badge to let the user know if the browser rejected their input (such as when running on an older Chrome version or using invalid characters).

openQuestions.title: Add direct spec and MDN reference links
openQuestions.detail: To make this page a useful standalone developer utility, we should add a dedicated references section with direct links to the ChromeStatus entry and W3C CSS Values spec.

summary: An excellent, highly interactive CSS utility that helps developers build and copy correct request-modifier URL syntax. It conforms perfectly to rules, but would be even stronger with live browser parser validation (testing if the browser successfully parses the rule) and direct reference links.
